title: Deliciously Smoked Kenyan Chicken Kebabs
description: A funky and light Kenyan dish featuring cider and tandoor smoked chicken, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this recipe is raw-friendly and full of flavor. It combines the richness of chicken with the tanginess of cider and spices.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor or smoker to 250 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together cider vinegar, tandoor spice mix, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Place the skewers in the tandoor or smoker and smoke for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, brush the chicken with cider and continue smoking for another 10 minutes.
Remove the skewers from the tandoor or smoker and let them rest for 5 minutes.
Sprinkle the chopped cilantro over the kebabs and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
